meta_title: For Students: deployment workflow to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to deployment workflow and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Deployment Workflow for Students: Master SaaS Launches
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no SaaS knowledge' barrier. Use this intermediate deployment workflow guide featuring Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first app.
content.answer_block: Transform your local prototype into a live production SaaS by automating environment configuration and deployment pipelines. By leveraging Antigravity for infrastructure-as-code and Claude Code for iterative CLI-based development, you bridge the gap between student projects and professional-grade software, ensuring your build is scalable, secure, and ready for real-world traffic.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks grinding in your IDE, perfecting the logic of your first AI application. Yet, the screen remains dark; the code is trapped in a local environment, invisible to the world. The transition from 'student project' to 'SaaS product' is where most developers fail, paralyzed by the complexity of server management and deployment pipelines.

But this barrier is merely a threshold. By mastering a standardized deployment workflow, you stop being a student of code and start becoming an architect of products. We are going to dismantle the mystery of production, using modern tooling to turn your repository into a live, scalable web presence.

steps.title: Environment Containerization
steps.description: Ensure your app runs identically on your machine and the server by defining a robust Docker environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a multi-stage Dockerfile for a Node.js/Python backend. Include production-ready security optimizations, non-root user configuration, and layer caching for fast builds.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a production-ready Dockerfile for this project repository'

steps.title: CI/CD Pipeline Automation
steps.description: Automate testing and build triggers so every 'git push' leads to a verified deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow that runs unit tests, performs static analysis, and builds the Docker image only on merge to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize a standard GitHub Actions CI pipeline with linting and build steps'

steps.title: Infrastructure Provisioning
steps.description: Use IaC to define your cloud resources, ensuring your environment is reproducible and version-controlled.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Terraform configuration to provision a scalable cloud instance, security groups, and an environment variable manager.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate terraform scripts for a basic cloud deployment architecture'

steps.title: Database Migration Strategy
steps.description: Handle data schema changes without downtime or corruption during your deployment cycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a migration script template for my database schema that supports rollbacks and zero-downtime updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'set up a database migration workflow using current drivers'

steps.title: Production Monitoring Setup
steps.description: Implement basic health checks and logging so you know exactly when a deployment succeeds or fails.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure an automated health check endpoint and integrate simple log streaming into the deployment pipeline.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add a health check endpoint and integrate logging middleware'

pro_tips: Always use environment variable masking in your CI/CD pipeline to prevent secrets from leaking into logs.
Implement a 'staging' environment that mirrors production exactly; never test your first deployment on live users.
Use ephemeral preview environments for pull requests to test features in a live browser context before merging.
Keep your Docker images lean by using Alpine Linux base images to reduce attack surfaces and deployment times.
Configure automated alerts for high CPU usage or failed health checks so you can fix bugs before users report them.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, focus on setting up a custom domain and implementing SSL via Cloudflare or AWS Certificate Manager to add the final layer of professional polish.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to move beyond local host and launch your vision to the world. Don't let your code sit idle in a repo; deploy it, iterate, and refine your craft. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
